headline: How major US stock indexes fared Thursday 5/28/2026
summary: U.S. stocks pushed to more records as companies like Dollar Tree, Snowflake and Hormel Foods kept piling up profits.  The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose less than 0.1%, and the Nasdaq composite climbed 0.9%, also adding to their own records from a day earlier.  The Nasdaq composite rose 242.74 points, or 0.9%, to 26,917.47.

headline: Dollar Tree Stock Surges After Earnings. The Retailer Has a Big New Delivery Partner.
summary: The company posted adjusted earnings of $1.74 a share for its fiscal first quarter, up from $1.26 last year and above analysts’ consensus estimate of $1.53, according to FactSet. headline: Dollar Tree (NASDAQ:DLTR) Reports Q1 CY2026 In Line With Expectations, Stock Jumps 10.4%
summary: Discount treasure-hunt retailer Dollar Tree (NASDAQ:DLTR) met Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 7.2% year on year to $4.98 billion. The company expects next quarter’s revenue to be around $4.85 billion, close to analysts’ estimates. Its non-GAAP profit of $1.74 per share was 12.6% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Oil prices climb, but US stocks hold near their records
summary: Oil prices are clawing back some of their sharp drops from earlier in the week on Thursday, but U.S. stocks are remaining near their records as companies like Dollar Tree, Snowflake and Hormel Foods keep piling up profits.  Stocks appear to be less beholden to swings in the oil market, where prices climbed Thursday following the latest threat to the ceasefire in the United States’ war with Iran.  U.S. Central Command said Kuwait had intercepted missiles launched by Iran late Wednesday night, following earlier “defensive” strikes by the U.S. military on missile launch sites and minelaying boats in southern Iran.
